_frame_metadata.purpose: Sub-Task tracking for the project (SubFrame's task system)
_frame_metadata.forAI: Auto-generated from .subframe/tasks/*.md — edit the .md files directly.

pending.title: Settings>AI Tool>Start Command - Enhance with Quick Badges
pending.description: In Settings for our AI Tool we allow setting the default start command, etc. It would be nice if common usages were below the input field as pills - respective to the AI tool used? For example Claude could have 'claude', or 'claude --dangerously-skip-permissions', etc.

pending.title: Overview Navbar status badge
pending.description: Enhance the Overview tab button in the terminal navbar to show a subtle status indicator that reflects the health status of the project and for example if subframe features need updating or other project health notifciations/warnings/etc. Perhaps this can include a section in the Overview view for this as well.

pending.title: Add "Agent Speak" - TTS functionality for agent output.
pending.description: I'd like to add an optional feature/enhancement to enable TTS for agent output available via local and paid models - similar to Voquil OSS software for ref. I'd like to add optional opt-in per terminal with state saving for lifecycle of window session for now. This should be agent agnostic but we will focus on Claude and getting that working at first. This should have similar UI features to maintain consistancy as the other aspects of our app. Allowing the user to set similar to voquil AI rewording/fixing optionally and releated feature/functionality in MVP to allow for prompt man-in-middle catch of raw output/re-transcribe to desired output-style.

pending.title: Plugin/Integrations system?
pending.description: Certain features/functionalitys may be desired but not neccesarrily be in the general scope/needed for all installations?

pending.title: Research better method for Usage Stats
pending.description: Our current approach for Usage Stats (at least for Claude) works - however it results in frequent 'Usage Unavailable'. We need to research this and ensure we're using the best approach.

pending.title: Add granular agent config management and global AI tool settings panel
pending.description: Extend SubFrame's Health/Overview panel into a full Agent Settings view where users can granularly include/exclude SubFrame components (hooks, knowledge, skills, plugins) during init and post-init, preview component contents in a detail view for transparency, and detect/manage global AI tool configuration files (claude global settings, CLAUDE.md, Gemini/Codex equivalents) as a central management hub.
pending.acceptanceCriteria:
- Users can toggle individual SubFrame components (hooks, knowledge files, skills, plugins) on/off during project init
- Users can enable/disable individual components after init from a dedicated settings panel
- Each component (skill, plugin, hook, knowledge file) has a 'preview' action that opens a full detail view showing its contents
- SubFrame detects global AI tool config files (e.g. ~/.claude/settings.json, global CLAUDE.md, .gemini/, .codex/) and displays their status
- Users can view and edit detected global AI tool configs from within SubFrame
- Existing Health Panel functionality is preserved and accessible from the new view
- All new IPC channels are typed in ipcChannels.ts and follow existing patterns

steps.label: Research and catalog all manageable SubFrame components and global AI config file paths across Claude, Gemini, and Codex
Add IPC channels and main-process logic to detect, read, and write global AI tool config files
Build Agent Settings panel UI with component toggle list (include/exclude) and detail preview drawer
Integrate granular component selection into the project init flow (both UI dialog and CLI)
Add global AI tool config viewer/editor section to the Agent Settings panel

pending.title: Claude Code chat sidebar
pending.description: Dedicated UI for Claude Code interactions

pending.title: Implement Windows code signing for installer and executable
pending.description: Obtain and integrate a Windows code signing certificate (EV or standard) into the build/release pipeline to eliminate SmartScreen warnings when users download and install SubFrame. This is critical for public launch credibility and user trust.
pending.acceptanceCriteria:
- A valid Windows code signing certificate is obtained from a trusted CA
- The Electron installer (.exe/.msi) and main executable are signed during the build process
- SmartScreen no longer shows 'unknown publisher' warnings on fresh installs
- Code signing is integrated into the GitHub Actions release workflow (automated)
- Signature can be verified via right-click → Properties → Digital Signatures on Windows
- Certificate renewal process and credentials storage are documented
pending.notes: [2026-03-14] Blocker for public release — unsigned apps trigger Windows SmartScreen warnings

steps.label: Research and select a code signing certificate provider (DigiCert, Sectigo, SSL.com EV vs OV)
Purchase certificate and complete identity verification process
Configure electron-builder signing options (win.sign / certificateFile / certificatePassword)
Integrate signing into GitHub Actions release workflow with secure credential storage (secrets)
Test signed installer on clean Windows machine and verify SmartScreen acceptance
Document certificate renewal timeline, credential rotation, and signing configuration

pending.title: First-launch onboarding wizard
pending.description: 3-step wizard on first launch: select AI tool → set project directory → open first project. Reduces drop-off for new users who don't know where to start.
pending.notes: [2026-03-14] Critical for first impressions — new users need guided setup

pending.title: Workspace backup and export
pending.description: Allow users to export workspace config, tasks, and session history as a zip. Provides data portability and peace of mind.

pending.title: Transport abstraction layer (IPC → pluggable)
pending.description: Refactor src/renderer/lib/ipc.ts to use a pluggable Transport interface. Extract ElectronTransport (current window.electron.invoke) and prepare WebSocketTransport stub for future browser mode. useIpcQuery/useIpcMutation stay unchanged — only the underlying transport swaps. This unlocks: CDN-loaded renderer for instant UI updates in Electron, browser mode via subframe-server, and mobile/tablet access. Depends on subframe-server being ready for the WebSocket side.

pending.title: Build UX audit skill
pending.description: Create a /ux-audit skill that performs a structured heuristic evaluation of SubFrame. Should check: shortcut conflicts, missing tooltips, dead-end states, label/terminology consistency, unreachable features, empty states, error states, accessibility gaps, command palette completeness, and flow coherence. Output a structured report with severity ratings.

pending.title: Enhanced agent activity view with session differentiation
pending.description: Overhaul AgentStateView to better differentiate separate Claude Code sessions and add visual hierarchy for sub-agents.
pending.acceptanceCriteria:
- Panel mode shows session sidebar + timeline (not just a single card)
- Sub-agents visually nested under parent sessions
- Multiple concurrent sessions clearly differentiated
- SidebarAgentStatus shows session count badge when >1 active

pending.title: Agent persona visualization — avatar cards, sub-agent tree, and task/tool map
pending.description: Add a visual personification view for agents that makes multi-session and sub-agent workflows tangible and intuitive.
pending.acceptanceCriteria:
- Agent cards render with status-colored aura and tool badge
- Sub-agent hierarchy visually represented as a tree
- Multiple sessions render side-by-side with clear boundaries
- Smooth animations for agent spawn/complete lifecycle
- Works in both sidebar (compact) and full-view modes

pending.title: Claude analytics dashboard — sessions, tokens, cost, usage
pending.description: Embed a Claud-ometer-style analytics dashboard directly in SubFrame. Reads `~/.claude/` data (session .jsonl logs, stats-cache.json, history.jsonl) to show usage, costs, token breakdowns, and session history — no external server needed.
pending.acceptanceCriteria:
- Overview shows total sessions, messages, tokens, cost
- At least one chart (usage over time or model breakdown)
- Session list with sort/filter
- Cost estimates using per-model pricing (input/output/cache tiers)
- Scopes to current project when project is selected
- Handles missing ~/.claude/ gracefully (empty state)

pending.title: Git integration and workflow configuration
pending.description: Implement git hook integration: optional pre-push hook that triggers pipeline runs, worktree isolation for mutable stages (via gitBranchesManager.addWorktree), branch filtering from workflow YAML (on.push.branches glob matching). Add WorkflowEditor component using CodeMirror for YAML editing. Store workflows per-project in .subframe/workflows/. Add default review.yml workflow template to frameTemplates.ts. See ADR-007 Sections 3.6-3.7.

pending.title: Pipeline extensibility — custom stages, env vars, artifact output
pending.description: Enable custom stage support: 'run:' shell commands and 'uses:' script references in workflow YAML. Expose stage environment variables (PIPELINE_RUN_ID, PIPELINE_BRANCH, PIPELINE_BASE_SHA, PIPELINE_HEAD_SHA, PIPELINE_WORKTREE, PIPELINE_ARTIFACTS, PIPELINE_PROJECT_ROOT, PIPELINE_AI_TOOL). Implement file-based artifact output (custom stages write to PIPELINE_ARTIFACTS/content/*.md, comments/*.json, patches/*.json). Parallel job execution via DAG topological sort. See ADR-007 Sections 3.7-3.8.

pending.title: Automated changelog generation from sub-task system
pending.description: Integrate CHANGELOG.md (keepachangelog spec) with the SubFrame sub-task and release workflow. Goals: (1) Auto-generate changelog entries from completed sub-tasks on release — map task categories to keepachangelog sections (feature→Added, fix→Fixed, etc.). (2) Add a /changelog skill that previews unreleased changes from completed tasks since last tag. (3) Update /release skill to append to CHANGELOG.md during version bump. (4) Consider rendering CHANGELOG.md inline in the Settings About tab (currently opens GitHub). (5) Keep .subframe/docs-internal/changelog.md as the detailed internal log — CHANGELOG.md is the user-facing keepachangelog spec file.

pending.title: Pipeline: multi-agent orchestration
pending.description: Implement stage-isolated agent invocations where each pipeline stage spawns a separate AI tool session with scoped context. Supports composable agent chains where output artifacts from one stage feed into the next stage's prompt context.

pending.title: Pipeline: release automation workflow
pending.description: Create a release pipeline workflow template that runs test suite, generates release notes via AI, bumps version, requires human approval gate, then pushes and creates GitHub release. Replaces the manual /release skill workflow with an automated pipeline.

pending.title: Pipeline: custom team workflow templates
pending.description: Add a workflow template gallery/browser in the Pipeline panel UI. Include starter templates for common workflows: database migration verification, Docker image builds, deployment to staging, security scanning (npm audit), and allow teams to create/share custom templates.

pending.title: Pipeline: parallel job execution
pending.description: Implement concurrent execution of independent jobs in the pipeline DAG. Jobs with no mutual dependencies (as determined by topological sort) should run in parallel using Promise.all. Includes progress tracking for parallel stages and proper abort propagation.

pending.title: Pipeline: worktree isolation for stages
pending.description: Implement git worktree-based isolation for pipeline stages. Create a temporary worktree for the pipeline run so stages can modify files without affecting the user's working directory. The worktreePath field already exists in StageContext but is always null.

pending.title: Research Claude Code remote/headless mode for session enhancement
pending.description: Investigate Claude Code's recently released remote code execution feature. Explore its capabilities, API surface, and how SubFrame could integrate with it to build enhanced session management — potentially enabling richer session context, persistent agent state, or background task execution beyond the local PTY model.

pending.title: Hook-side terminal ID injection for session correlation
pending.description: Have Claude hook scripts (pre-tool-use.js, post-tool-use.js) write terminal PID or env var into agent-state.json session entries, enabling deterministic terminal↔session mapping instead of timestamp heuristic.

pending.title: Agent state session cleanup / rotation
pending.description: agent-state.json accumulates sessions indefinitely (currently 3300+ lines). Add rotation logic: archive completed sessions older than N days, cap max sessions, or prune on startup.
pending.acceptanceCriteria: agent-state.json stays bounded; old completed sessions are pruned or archived

pending.title: Test onboarding analysis flow with Codex and Gemini CLI
pending.description: Verify the onboarding AI analysis pipeline works correctly with non-Claude tools (Codex, Gemini CLI). These still use the pipe/tee approach since they may not support interactive mode. Confirm: streaming output appears, sentinel detection works, timeout is sufficient, and parseAnalysisResponse handles their output formats.
pending.userRequest: Add a sub-task to test/verify this with codex/gemini
pending.acceptanceCriteria: Analysis completes successfully with Codex CLI,Analysis completes successfully with Gemini CLI,Output is parsed correctly from tee file,Timeout does not fire prematurely,Error patterns detected correctly for each tool

pending.title: Expand test coverage for core managers
pending.description: The project has Vitest configured but main process managers (ptyManager, tasksManager, settingsManager, agentStateManager) likely need more unit test coverage. Add tests for IPC handler logic, task CRUD operations, and markdown parser edge cases to prevent regressions.

pending.title: Cross-platform testing and CI for macOS and Linux
pending.description: README states macOS and Linux builds are generated but untested. Add CI matrix builds for all three platforms and document platform-specific prerequisites and known issues to support the community testing effort.

pending.title: Add markdown preview to task description field
pending.description: Task descriptions support markdown at the storage/parser level (taskMarkdownParser.ts) and MarkdownPreview component exists, but the task dialog uses a plain textarea. Integrate a split-view or toggle preview using the existing MarkdownPreview component for richer task descriptions.

pending.title: Document IPC channel contract and data flow patterns
pending.description: While ipcChannels.ts serves as the source of truth for channel definitions, add developer documentation (in .subframe/docs-internal/) explaining the end-to-end IPC pattern: channel definition → main handler → renderer hook → component usage, with examples for adding new channels.

pending.title: E2E testing with Playwright/Electron
pending.description: Set up end-to-end testing for SubFrame using Playwright with Electron support. Cover critical user flows: project switching, terminal creation, task management, settings persistence. This catches runtime/visual regressions that unit tests miss — especially important for dependency updates that pass typecheck but break UI behavior.

pending.title: Expand unit test coverage for renderer hooks and components
pending.description: Current test suite (190 tests) covers main-process logic well but has no renderer-side tests. Add unit tests for critical hooks (useTasks, useSettings, useSubFrameHealth) and key components (ViewTabBar, TasksPanel, RightPanel). Use React Testing Library + Vitest. Goal: catch prop/state regressions from dependency updates before they reach production.

pending.title: Upgrade @eslint/js to v10 (coordinated eslint ecosystem bump)
pending.description: Dependabot PR #14 closed — @eslint/js 9→10 requires coordinated bump of eslint, @eslint/js, and typescript-eslint together due to peer dependency conflicts. Currently deferred in dependabot.yml ignore rules.
pending.userRequest: Deferred dependency upgrade identified during Dependabot PR triage
pending.acceptanceCriteria: All three packages upgraded together, eslint config updated if needed, npm run check passes, dependabot.yml ignore rule removed

steps.label: Check latest compatible versions of eslint, @eslint/js, typescript-eslint
Update all three packages simultaneously
Fix any eslint config breaking changes
Run npm run check — all gates pass
Remove @eslint/js ignore rule from dependabot.yml

pending.title: Upgrade recharts to v3 (breaking API migration)
pending.description: Dependabot PR #11 closed — recharts 2→3 has breaking API changes affecting src/renderer/components/ui/chart.tsx. Tooltip payload/label props removed from type, legend payload type changed. Currently deferred in dependabot.yml ignore rules.
pending.userRequest: Deferred dependency upgrade identified during Dependabot PR triage
pending.acceptanceCriteria: recharts upgraded to v3, chart.tsx updated for new API, all charts render correctly, npm run check passes, dependabot.yml ignore rule removed

steps.label: Review recharts v3 migration guide
Audit chart.tsx for all breaking API usage
Update chart.tsx tooltip and legend types/props
Visually verify all charts render correctly
Run npm run check — all gates pass
Remove recharts ignore rule from dependabot.yml

pending.title: Persistent status bar mode (opt-in)
pending.description: Add an opt-in setting to make the ActivityBar always visible as a VS Code-style status bar. When enabled, shows git branch, active AI tool, usage stats, and activity streams in a persistent 24px bottom bar. When disabled (default), ActivityBar only appears when activity streams exist. This consolidates scattered status info (usage pill in tab bar, AI tool in settings) into one surface and provides a natural extension point for future integrations/plugins.

pending.title: CodeMirror theme reads CSS variables at runtime
pending.description: codemirror-theme.ts has 16+ hardcoded amber (#d4a574) values for syntax highlighting. Editor stays amber across all themes. Refactor to read --color-accent and other CSS variables at runtime and rebuild the CodeMirror theme when ThemeProvider changes them.

steps.label: Read CSS variables in codemirror-theme.ts instead of hardcoded hex
Use CodeMirror theme compartment for dynamic updates
Call theme refresh from ThemeProvider on settings change

pending.title: StructureMap D3 graph uses CSS variables for colors
pending.description: StructureMap.tsx has 14+ hardcoded amber and background color values in D3 SVG rendering. Module graph stays amber across all themes. Refactor MODULE_COLORS and inline styles to read from CSS variables.

steps.label: Replace MODULE_COLORS hardcoded values with CSS variable reads
Replace inline style background/text colors with CSS variable reads
Add runtime refresh when theme changes

pending.title: MarkdownPreview syntax highlighting respects theme
pending.description: MarkdownPreview.tsx has 2 hardcoded #d4a574 amber values in highlight.js inline CSS string. Code blocks in previews stay amber across all themes. Replace with CSS variable references.

steps.label: Replace hardcoded colors in highlight.js CSS with var() references
Verify rendering across all theme presets

pending.title: Add E2E tests for critical user flows
pending.description: The project has 781 unit tests but no end-to-end tests visible. Adding Playwright or Cypress tests for critical flows (onboarding wizard, agent chat, theme switching, slash commands) would catch integration regressions that unit tests miss.

pending.title: Add error boundary components for graceful failure handling
pending.description: Chat, pipeline graph, and cost dashboard are complex UI surfaces that could benefit from React error boundaries to prevent full-page crashes when a single component fails (e.g., malformed SSE data, unexpected API responses).

pending.title: Migrate conversation storage from localStorage to IndexedDB
pending.description: localStorage has a ~5-10MB limit per origin. With base64 media attachments in conversations, power users could hit this limit. IndexedDB would provide much larger storage capacity and better performance for large conversation histories.

pending.title: Add accessibility audit and ARIA attributes
pending.description: The onboarding wizard, slash command autocomplete dropdown, pipeline graph, and kanban board likely need ARIA roles, keyboard navigation improvements, and screen reader support to meet WCAG guidelines.

pending.title: Add rate limiting and request deduplication to chat API routes
pending.description: The chat API route (POST /api/chat/[id]) and vision polling pipeline could benefit from request deduplication to prevent double-sends on rapid clicks, and basic rate limiting to avoid overwhelming the gateway during rapid interactions.

pending.title: Per-workspace UI state persistence
pending.description: When switching workspaces, UI state like active right panel, sidebar collapsed state, grid layout, and editor open files should be saved and restored per-workspace. Currently all UI state is global — switching from a workspace where you had GitHub panel open to one where you were using Sub-Tasks retains the old panel. Save UI preferences (activePanel, sidebarState, gridLayout, viewMode, editorOpenFiles) to the workspace data file and restore on switch.

steps.label: Add uiPreferences field to workspace data model
Save current UI state on workspace switch-away
Restore UI state on workspace switch-to
Handle missing preferences gracefully (first switch to a workspace)

completed.title: Task Editor UI Option
completed.description:
Currently the Sub-Tasks system only allows viewing existing tasks via the markdown editor. Similar to the creation wizard for Sub-Tasks, allow switching between UI/Markdown view.
I also want to enhance the Sub-Tasks side view pane dropdown detail view (and full view dropdown detail view) to visual render within dropdown (steps, timeline element, etc) if available.
Lastly I want to fix the Sub-Tasks Tags to properly split priority out into its own column so we can sort by priority as well in table view.
Lastly Lastly - I want to allow easy task reference in chat - I know we can send to chat but it would be nice to have a quick 'copy-id' or something of that nature to allow copying ref to task to chat with agent about, etc.
Lastly Lastly Lastly - allow user to easily via UI link tasks to be dependant/blocking/sequential etc and enhance UI to cleanly/properly show this - match our clean UI/UX - no skimpy.
Fix markdown/codemirror editor to allow proper text selection via cursor/mouse etc. currently glitchy.
Allow 'AI-Enhance' or whatever best name is for button to fix a usertyped task to properly scope/description/use sub-task features/etc. this should be quick and allow model choice (default to sonnet?) - leverage existing utilities if possible/correct.
completed.notes:
Any additional context or references.
[2026-03-10] Split priority into its own sortable column in panel mode (was merged in Tags). Default sort now: status asc + priority high→low. Both category and priority columns are sortable with multi-sort.
[2026-03-10] Implemented enhanced TaskDetail (two-column layout, progress bar, copy ID, user request display, dependency resolution with icons) and dependency linking UI in dialog Advanced section
[2026-03-10] Task editing UI was already functional (Form/Markdown toggle, steps editor, priority/category/status). Now enhanced with dependency linking (blockedBy/blocks) in Advanced section.
[2026-03-10] Fixed CodeMirror text selection in Editor and WorkflowEditor by adding onOpenAutoFocus/onPointerDownOutside preventDefault to DialogContent — prevents Radix focus trap from interfering with CM mouse events
[2026-03-10] Added AI-Enhance button: new IPC channel ENHANCE_TASK, handler in tasksManager spawns active AI tool with structured prompt, returns enhanced title/description/steps/criteria/priority/category. UI shows Sparkles button in dialog header when title present.

steps.label: Enhance Sub-Task editing with UI capability
Enhance Sub-Task dropdown view with UI capability
Split priority into its own column in Sub-Tasks table view
AI-Enhance button to auto-scope tasks
Fix CodeMirror text selection
Dependency linking UI (blockedBy/blocks in dialog)
Copy task ID button for chat reference

completed.title: Left Sidebar 'Add Projects' via add new folder doesn't add to workspace
completed.description: The left sidebar 'Add Projects' discovery overlay doesn't allow refresh or when user adds project to workspace via Add Folder (File Explorer) nothing happens. Adding from 'Discovered' does work.

completed.title: Show Active Project/Workspace in Collapsed Left Sidebar
Allow Terminal windows to Pop-Out to a new window/pane.
Allow command pallet to access user saved Prompts
Fix Terminal Slot Retention
completed.description: Terminals seem to prefer their original slot as if we swap views or change grid layouts, termina's end up reordering back to their original order.

completed.title: Add protection to closing windows with agent running
Allow Sub-Tasks to be private
completed.description: Sub-Tasks should be allowed to be marked private and not tracked via git. All UI/Sub-Frame features should still work as normal otherwise. We should include enhancements to skils/hooks, etc if needed.

completed.title: IDE layout (sidebar + terminal)
completed.description: 3-panel IDE layout with project explorer, terminal, and history

completed.description: Hierarchical, collapsible file tree

completed.title: Prompt history panel
completed.description: Side panel showing command history with timestamps

completed.description: Support multiple terminal instances in tabs

completed.title: Terminal grid view
completed.description: Side-by-side terminal panels with grid layout (2x1, 2x2, 3x1, 3x2, 3x3)

completed.title: File editor overlay
completed.description: Basic file editing capability within the app

completed.title: Detect if Claude Code is actually running
completed.description: Currently we track claudeCodeRunning with a simple boolean. Improve detection to actually check if Claude Code is running in terminal (parse terminal output for Claude prompt patterns).

completed.title: CLAUDE.md sablonu ve task sistemi
completed.description: Frame projeleri icin CLAUDE.md sablonu, task tanima kurallari, dokuman guncelleme stratejileri

completed.title: Modern UI tasarimi
completed.description: Claude Desktop benzeri minimalist ve modern tasarim. DM Sans + JetBrains Mono fontlari, warm-neutral renk paleti.

completed.title: Research & implement Claude Code hooks for SubFrame
completed.context: Session 2026-02-28 - CLAUDE.md rewrite and context loading discussion
completed.description: Research Claude Code's hooks system (startup hooks, pre/post command hooks, etc.) and evaluate how SubFrame can leverage them to enforce context loading at session start. Currently CLAUDE.md instructs Claude to read STRUCTURE.json, tasks.json, and PROJECT_NOTES.md — but this is a soft directive. A startup hook could automatically dump key context or run find-module.js on init. Scope: (1) Research available hook types and trigger points. (2) Prototype a startup hook that auto-loads SubFrame context files. (3) Evaluate whether hooks can be bundled with SubFrame project initialization (frameProject.js / projectInit.js). (4) Document findings and recommended hook configurations in AGENTS.md or a new docs file.
completed.userRequest: User noted that CLAUDE.md 'read at session start' is a directive, not enforced. Suggested hooks as a way to make context loading bulletproof. User said: 'add hooks enhancements/research as a task/todo in our system'
completed.acceptanceCriteria: 1. Documented research on Claude Code hooks API (what's available, how they work). 2. Working prototype of a SubFrame startup hook that auto-loads context. 3. Decision on whether to include hooks in SubFrame project init. 4. If implemented, hooks are registered during Initialize as SubFrame Project flow.
completed.notes:
Claude Code supports hooks in ~/.claude/settings.json and .claude/settings.json (project-scoped). Could also explore /init command integration. Related to the broader goal of making SubFrame context loading guaranteed rather than best-effort.
[2026-03-01] Implemented: CLI script (scripts/task.js), 3 hooks (session-start, prompt-submit, stop), /sub-tasks skill, archive system. All documented in CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

completed.title: Git status integration
completed.description: Show git status, diff in the UI

completed.title: Settings panel
completed.description: UI for configuring preferences

completed.title: Deploy hooks/CLI/skill to initialized SubFrame projects
completed.description: Currently, the Sub-Task automation (scripts/task.js CLI, scripts/hooks/*.js hooks, .claude/skills/sub-tasks/ skill, .claude/settings.json hook config) only exists in the SubFrame repo itself. Projects initialized via 'Initialize as SubFrame Project' get tasks.json and docs but not the automated tooling. Add an optional step to initializeProject() in projectInit.ts that deploys lightweight versions of: (1) scripts/task.js CLI script, (2) scripts/hooks/ session-start, prompt-submit, stop hooks, (3) .claude/settings.json hook wiring, (4) .claude/skills/sub-tasks/SKILL.md skill. Consider whether to copy files or generate from templates. Must not overwrite existing .claude/settings.json content (merge hook config).
completed.userRequest: Will this get 'Installed' when we init a SubFrame project?

completed.title: Add clean uninitialize/cleanup flow for SubFrame projects
completed.description: No uninit/cleanup flow exists. Add the ability to cleanly remove SubFrame from a project without breaking anything. Should remove: .subframe/ directory, AGENTS.md (if SubFrame-generated), CLAUDE.md/GEMINI.md backlinks (preserve user content), .githooks/ (if SubFrame-created), .claude/settings.json hook entries (if SubFrame-added), .claude/skills/sub-tasks/ (if deployed). Must be safe — warn before destructive actions, confirm with user, never delete user content. Could be a CLI command (node scripts/init.js --uninit) and/or a UI button in SubFrame app.
completed.userRequest: Do we allow cleanly and properly uninitializing a project/cleanup without breaking?

completed.title: Show SubFrame initialization status cleanly in UI
completed.description: Currently project detection is binary — isFrameProject() checks .subframe/config.json existence. Enhance to show richer status: (1) Which SubFrame components are present (config, tasks, structure, hooks, CLI, skill). (2) Visual indicator in project list showing init level (e.g., 'basic' vs 'full' with hooks). (3) Ability to see what's missing and optionally install missing components. Could be a status panel or info tooltip expansion in the project list UI.

completed.title: Floating command palette (Ctrl+/)
completed.description: Create a clean UI/UX floating command palette triggered by Ctrl+/. Should allow quick access to actions, panel toggles, terminal commands, project switching, and settings. Inspired by VS Code's command palette but tailored to SubFrame's feature set.

completed.title: Documentation audit, quality infrastructure & code review
completed.description: Full session: (1) Audit all docs vs code, compare with Axiom-Portal patterns. (2) Populate docs-internal/ with ADRs, architecture, changelog, IPC ref. (3) Add Vitest test suite (36 tests), ESLint 9 + Prettier, GitHub Actions CI, pre-commit hook. (4) Update update-structure.js for TypeScript support (ESM imports, generics, path-segment exclusion). (5) Code review: fixed 13 issues (vitest ESM config, tsconfig.test.json, function regex generics, isExcluded path matching, frameConstants.js sync, IPC ref doc 93→112 channels, etc.).

completed.title: Prompt library overlay with smart search and terminal insert
completed.description:
Command-palette-style overlay for saved/common prompts. Triggered by keyboard shortcut, fuzzy searches across title + content + tags, and pastes selected prompt into the active terminal via PTY write.

**Core features:**
- Overlay UI (Command palette pattern via shadcn Command component)
- Fuzzy search across title, prompt content, and tags
- Insert into active terminal on select (PTY write)
- CRUD: add/edit/delete prompts from overlay + dedicated management view
- Categories/tags for organization

**Storage:**
- Project-level: .subframe/prompts.json
- Optional global: ~/.subframe/prompts.json (shared across projects)

**Nice-to-have:**
- Template variables ({{project}}, {{file}}) that resolve on insert
- Import/export prompt collections
- Usage frequency tracking for smart sorting

completed.title: CodeMirror 6 editor integration
completed.description: Replace textarea editor with CodeMirror 6. Full features: syntax highlighting, minimap, autocomplete, linting, find/replace, multi-cursor, code folding, bracket matching. Custom SubFrame dark theme. Update all docs.

completed.title: File preview system — VS Code-style renderer
completed.description: Add a preview/render mode to the editor for non-code files. Support: Markdown (rendered HTML), HTML/CSS (live preview), SVG (inline render), images (display with zoom/pan). Toggle between edit mode (CodeMirror) and preview mode. Architecture: preview renderer component that switches based on file type. Research needed: best markdown renderer (react-markdown vs marked vs mdx), HTML sandboxing (iframe vs shadow DOM), SVG inline rendering approach.

completed.title: Research file preview/renderer libraries
completed.description: Evaluate libraries for rendering file previews in SubFrame's editor dialog. Must work with React 19 + TypeScript + esbuild + Electron. Research: (1) Markdown: react-markdown, marked, remark/rehype, mdx — need GFM support, code block syntax highlighting (can reuse CM6/Shiki), math rendering; (2) HTML/CSS: iframe sandbox vs shadow DOM vs srcDoc approach — security considerations in Electron; (3) SVG: inline rendering via dangerouslySetInnerHTML vs react-svg vs object tag; (4) Images: native img tag with zoom/pan (react-zoom-pan-pinch or similar). Compare bundle size, React 19 compat, maintenance status. Recommend the best option for each file type.

completed.title: Markdown preview toggle mode
completed.description: Add edit/preview toggle to the editor dialog for .md/.markdown files. When in preview mode, render markdown as styled HTML matching SubFrame's dark theme. Features: GFM (tables, task lists, strikethrough), syntax-highlighted code blocks, heading anchors, image rendering. Toggle button in editor header bar next to Save/Close. Preserve scroll position when toggling. Preview should be read-only scrollable container with SubFrame typography.

completed.title: HTML/CSS live preview mode
completed.description: Add preview mode for .html/.htm/.css files in the editor dialog. Render HTML in a sandboxed iframe (srcDoc approach) with live updates as the user edits. For CSS files, show a preview pane with sample HTML that applies the stylesheet. Security: sandbox attribute on iframe to prevent script execution unless user opts in. Match SubFrame's dark background around the preview frame.

completed.title: SVG and image preview
completed.description: Add preview/display for SVG and image files (.svg, .png, .jpg, .gif, .webp, .bmp, .ico). SVGs: render inline with proper scaling, show source code toggle. Images: display with fit-to-container scaling, zoom/pan capability. For binary images, modify fileEditor.ts to return a base64 data URI or file:// path instead of text content. For SVGs (which are text), support both rendered view and source code editing.

completed.title: Real-time agent state visualization
completed.description: Live monitoring of Claude Code agent sessions in SubFrame. Ports & Adapters architecture: Claude Code PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks write to .subframe/agent-state.json, main process watches via fs.watch, renderer displays via AgentStateView/AgentTimeline/SidebarAgentStatus. 4 new IPC channels, Ctrl+Shift+A shortcut, terminal navbar Agents button.
completed.userRequest: Enhance Sub-Tasks with sidebar-timeline/stepper, full view, real-time agent/session visualization inspired by claude-code-by-agents project

completed.title: Deploy Claude Code skills with SubFrame init
completed.description: Make /sub-tasks, /sub-docs, /sub-audit skills first-class deployable components — tracked by health panel, deployed during init, updatable, and cleanly removable on uninstall. Deployed versions use direct file manipulation instead of SubFrame-app-specific CLI.

completed.title: Project onboarding & AI analysis pipeline
completed.description: Detect existing project intelligence files, run AI analysis through visible terminal tab, import structured results into SubFrame spec files. Reusable runAnalysisInTerminal() pipeline.

completed.title: Electron auto-updater
completed.description: Implement self-updating via electron-updater. Check GitHub Releases for new versions, show in-app notification, one-click install. Use delta updates to minimize download size. This is the immediate solution — the transport abstraction sub-task covers the future CDN-loaded renderer approach for browser mode.
completed.notes:
[2026-03-02] Implementing standard electron-updater first. Transport abstraction (for future browser mode) tracked separately.
[2026-03-03] Implemented electron-updater auto-update system: updaterManager (main), useUpdater hook (renderer), UpdateNotification toast component, SettingsPanel 'Check for Updates' button, 5 IPC channels. Dev mode guard prevents crashes in development. Build and typecheck pass.

completed.title: In-app changelog / What's New
completed.description: Show What's New dialog after updates. Surface changelog content from docs-internal/changelog.md or RELEASE_NOTES.md. Helps users discover new features after auto-update.

completed.description: Rewrite getAgentsTemplate() in frameTemplates.ts and .js with correct YAML frontmatter format, hooks docs, skills docs, session start guidance, docs-internal reference, core working principle, and before-ending-work checklist. No SubFrame-internal references (scripts/task.js, find-module.js). Target ~350-400 lines.

Mirror the sidebar AI status display. 2. Review empty grid cell UX in TerminalGrid.tsx — consider whether the current Plus button is discoverable enough or needs enhancement.

completed.title: Pipeline engine foundation — data model, IPC, and executor
completed.description: Implement the core pipeline infrastructure: data types in ipcChannels.ts (PipelineRun, PipelineJob, PipelineStage, PipelineArtifact types), IPC channels (PIPELINE_START, PIPELINE_CANCEL, PIPELINE_PROGRESS, etc.), pipelineManager.ts with init()+setupIPC(), workflow YAML parser, sequential stage executor with DAG support, and artifact collection system. Register in main/index.ts. See ADR-007 Section 3.1-3.3.

completed.title: Built-in pipeline stages — lint, test, describe, critique, freeze
completed.description: Implement the built-in stage handlers in pipelineStages.ts: critique (AI code review → CommentArtifacts), lint (discover+run linters → PatchArtifacts with cached lint.sh), describe (PR title+body+Mermaid → ContentArtifact), test (run/generate tests → ContentArtifact with verdict), freeze (auto-apply pre-freeze patches, lock worktree), push (git push via gitBranchesManager), create-pr (gh pr create from accumulated artifacts). Each handler follows the StageHandler interface. See ADR-007 Section 3.4.

completed.title: Pipeline review panel — UI for runs, artifacts, and approvals
completed.description: Build the renderer components: PipelinePanel.tsx (panel+full-view modes with run list and detail), PipelineTimeline.tsx (horizontal stage flow with animated status), usePipeline.ts hook (follows useTasks pattern — push listener, staleTime:Infinity, stable mutation refs), CritiqueView.tsx (file-grouped CommentArtifacts with severity badges), PatchReview.tsx (diff display with checkbox select and Apply button), PipelineLogView.tsx (scrollable per-stage logs). Add 'pipeline' to PanelId in useUIStore, wire into Sidebar+RightPanel+TerminalArea. Four tabs: Overview, Critique, Patches, Log. See ADR-007 Section 3.5.

completed.title: Pipeline engine foundation
completed.description: Data model, IPC channels, pipelineManager.ts, workflow parser, stage executor. ADR-007 Phase 1.

completed.title: Theme system with presets and custom themes
completed.description: Implement a full theme system that lets users: (1) choose from built-in presets (Classic Amber, Synthwave Traces, custom), (2) customize individual tokens (accent, neon traces, surfaces), (3) save/load custom themes. Includes: theme data model in settings, ThemeProvider component, Appearance tab in SettingsPanel with color pickers, preset gallery, live preview, and runtime CSS variable injection. Neon synthwave traces (purple/pink/cyan) from the logo palette become configurable.

completed.title: Pipeline workflow editor UI with visual builder and YAML view
completed.description: Add a workflow editor to PipelinePanel with: 1) Visual builder — step list with add/remove/reorder, autofill for uses: (lint/test/describe/critique/freeze/push), with: config fields (scope, mode, focus, prompt), run: command input, approval/continue-on-error toggles. 2) YAML view — CodeMirror editor with YAML syntax highlighting, toggle between visual and YAML. 3) Save/create/delete workflows via IPC. 4) New workflow dialog with template presets. Modern UI with animations matching SubFrame design system.

Add build pipeline safety (verify:hooks in check/CI/pre-commit). Ensures projects initialized with SubFrame can be reliably updated when SubFrame itself is updated.

completed.title: Enhance auto-update check interval and strategy
completed.description: Current default check interval is 4 hours which is too long for a fast-moving beta. Consider: shorter default interval (30min-1hr) during beta, configurable interval in settings (already exists but default too high), check-on-focus (check when app regains focus after being backgrounded, like VS Code), check-on-push (if we detect a git push in the project, trigger a check), exponential backoff on repeated 'up to date' responses. Also evaluate whether electron-updater's differential downloads are working — full re-download on every patch is wasteful.

steps.label: Research electron-updater best practices for beta update frequency
Lower default check interval to 1hr for beta builds
Add check-on-focus trigger (app.on focus event)
Evaluate differential/delta updates

completed.title: Audit and fix long-running AI task UX (dialog/activity bar coupling)
completed.description: Modal dialogs that initiate background AI operations (Enhance Sub-Task, Onboarding Analysis) break UX when closed — the operation continues in the background (visible in ActivityBar) but the user can't reopen the dialog to see current state/results. Need to either: (1) make dialogs resumable by linking to in-progress operations, (2) move long-running operations out of modals into persistent panels, or (3) add a global 'running operations' view that shows results. Pipelines are fine (persistent panel). The ActivityBar infrastructure is solid — the flaw is purely at the UI coupling layer.
completed.userRequest: Ensure all long-running AI tasks properly use global activity system AND that UI supports reopening/reattaching to in-progress operations
completed.notes:
[2026-03-16] Implemented Option C (hybrid): pendingEnhance in Zustand store survives dialog close, toast with View Results action reopens dialog pre-populated. Also fixed: SettingsPanel CLI install/uninstall loading states. Audit found: CLI install (high), SCAN_PROJECT_DIR (medium), RECHECK_AI_TOOLS (medium) — first two fixed, third already had spinner.
[2026-03-16] Audit pass 2: Fixed 5 additional issues — View Results navigates to Tasks panel, pendingEnhance cleared on form submit, Create/Update disabled during enhance, SCAN_PROJECT_DIR loading state added. OnboardingDialog confirmed clean (has cancel-on-close logic). ActivityBar cancel does not interact with pendingEnhance (acceptable).

steps.label: Audit all long-running operation entry points (Enhance, Onboarding, Pipeline, AI Tool)
Map each operation's lifecycle: initiation → progress → completion → result delivery
Design UX pattern for resumable/persistent background operations
Implement operation-to-UI linking (e.g. store active operation IDs, reattach on dialog open)
Test: close dialog during Enhance, reopen, verify state restoration
Test: navigate away during pipeline, return, verify state persists

(10) Cross-platform Dev Tooling - scripts/dev.js, DEV_SETUP.bat + dev_setup.ps1. (11) Docs - README.md and QUICKSTART.md updated with Windows setup, commands, roadmap. completed.title: Full UI refactor to React + TypeScript
completed.context: Session 2026-03-01 - Major architecture evolution from vanilla JS to React + TypeScript. Full plan in PROJECT_NOTES.md.
completed.description:
Complete refactor of SubFrame from vanilla JavaScript with imperative DOM manipulation to a modern React + TypeScript stack. This is a multi-phase effort covering the entire codebase.

**Technology Stack:**
- **React 19** — component architecture, hooks, Suspense for lazy-loaded panels
- **TypeScript** (strict mode) — type safety across main, renderer, shared, and IPC layer
- **Zustand** — lightweight global state (UI state: active panel, terminal IDs, sidebar width, view mode)
- **TanStack Query** — IPC call caching, loading/error states, invalidation (file tree, sessions, tasks, overview)
- **TanStack Table** — headless table for tasks list, sessions list with built-in sorting, filtering, column management
- **shadcn/ui** — component library built on Radix UI primitives (buttons, modals/dialogs, tabs, dropdowns, tooltips, context menus, command palette, forms, toasts). Implies **Tailwind CSS** as the styling system.
- **Tailwind CSS v4** — replaces the hand-written CSS files (variables.css, main.css, layout.css, panels.css, terminal.css, ui.css). SubFrame's warm-neutral + amber accent design tokens map to Tailwind theme config / shadcn CSS variables.
- **Framer Motion** — declarative animations for panel open/close, modal transitions, list reorder, AnimatePresence for exit animations, layout animations for sidebar resize.
- **esbuild** (kept) — already supports TSX natively, sub-second builds preserved.

**Scope:**

**Phase 1 — Foundation (TypeScript + Tailwind + Build)**
- Add `tsconfig.json` configs (separate for main, renderer, shared) with strict mode
- Update esbuild config for `.tsx` entry point and JSX transform
- Install and configure Tailwind CSS v4 with PostCSS (esbuild plugin or separate step)
- Initialize shadcn/ui (`npx shadcn@latest init`) — configure theme with SubFrame's warm-neutral palette (--background, --foreground, --accent mapped from current --bg-primary, --text-primary, --accent-primary #d4a574)
- Add dependencies: `react`, `react-dom`, `zustand`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `@tanstack/react-table`, `framer-motion`, `tailwindcss`, `@types/react`, `@types/react-dom`, `@types/node`, `typescript`, plus shadcn peer deps (`class-variance-authority`, `clsx`, `tailwind-merge`, `lucide-react`)
- Convert `src/shared/ipcChannels.js` → `ipcChannels.ts` with typed channel map (backbone — every IPC call becomes type-safe)
- Convert remaining shared modules: `frameConstants.ts`, `frameTemplates.ts`, `backlinkUtils.ts`, `projectInit.ts`
- Create typed IPC utility: `typedInvoke<Channel>(...args): Promise<ReturnType>` for renderer, `typedHandle<Channel>(handler)` for main
- Create TanStack Query wrapper hooks for IPC: `useIpcQuery(channel, args)` and `useIpcMutation(channel)`

**Phase 2 — Main Process (TypeScript only, no React)**
- Convert all 20 main process files from `.js` to `.ts`
- Add interfaces for all manager modules (e.g., `ISettingsManager`, `IWorkspace`, `IPtyManager`)
- Type all `ipcMain.handle` / `ipcMain.on` handlers with the typed IPC utility
- Type node-pty instances, Electron BrowserWindow, and menu builders
- Files: index.js, ptyManager.js, pty.js, workspace.js, settingsManager.js, tasksManager.js, pluginsManager.js, claudeSessionsManager.js, overviewManager.js, frameProject.js, fileTree.js, fileEditor.js, gitBranchesManager.js, githubManager.js, promptLogger.js, claudeUsageManager.js, aiToolManager.js, aiFilesManager.js, menu.js, dialogs.js

**Phase 3 — Renderer: React + shadcn Components**
- Replace `index.html` monolithic structure with a single `<div id='root'>` + React mount
- Set up providers: `<QueryClientProvider>` (TanStack Query), Zustand stores (no provider needed)
- Install shadcn components as needed: `button`, `dialog`, `tabs`, `dropdown-menu`, `context-menu`, `command`, `input`, `textarea`, `badge`, `tooltip`, `toast`/`sonner`, `separator`, `scroll-area`, `resizable` (for sidebar/panels), `sheet` (for mobile-style slide panels), `table`
- Create React component tree:
  - `<App>` — root layout with `<ResizablePanelGroup>` (shadcn resizable) for sidebar + main + right panel
  - `<Sidebar>` — workspace `<DropdownMenu>`, project list, action `<Button>`s
  - `<FileTree>` — recursive tree with expand/collapse via `<Collapsible>`, icons from `lucide-react`
  - `<TerminalArea>` — `<Tabs>` (shadcn) for tab bar + terminal grid container
  - `<Terminal>` — xterm.js wrapper with useEffect lifecycle, `<ContextMenu>` for right-click (shadcn)
  - `<Editor>` — file editor as `<Dialog>` or `<Sheet>` overlay
  - `<RightPanel>` — `<Tabs>` container for sub-panels
  - `<TasksPanel>` — TanStack Table for task list with sorting/filtering, `<Dialog>` for add/edit modal with shadcn form components, `<Badge>` for status/priority
  - `<SessionsPanel>` — TanStack Table for session list with sorting by date/status, split `<Button>` for resume
  - `<PluginsPanel>` — plugin cards
  - `<SettingsPanel>` — `<Dialog>` with `<Tabs>` sections (General, AI Tool, Terminal)
  - `<OverviewPanel>` — dashboard cards with `<motion.div>` entry animations
  - `<AIFilesPanel>` — status indicators + action `<Button>`s
  - `<GithubPanel>` — PR/issue list
  - `<ProjectListUI>` — project cards in sidebar
  - `<AIToolSelector>` — `<DropdownMenu>` or `<Command>` palette for tool selection
  - `<StructureMap>` — D3 visualization via useRef + useEffect (D3 manages its own DOM subtree)
- Zustand stores: `useUIStore` (sidebar state, active panel, view mode), `useProjectStore` (active project, workspace), `useTerminalStore` (terminal IDs, active terminal, grid layout)
- TanStack Query hooks: `useFileTree(projectPath)`, `useTasks(projectPath)`, `useSessions()`, `useOverview()`, `usePlugins()`, `useSettings()`
- TanStack Table instances: `useTasksTable()` with column defs for title/status/priority/category/date + sorting + filtering, `useSessionsTable()` with column defs for title/date/branch/messages/state + sorting
- Framer Motion: `<AnimatePresence>` for panel show/hide, `<motion.div>` with `layout` prop for sidebar resize, `<Reorder>` for draggable terminal tabs, staggered list animations for task/session entries

**Phase 4 — Styles Migration (CSS → Tailwind)**
- Delete hand-written CSS files: variables.css, main.css, layout.css, panels.css, terminal.css, ui.css
- Map current design tokens to Tailwind theme:
  - `--bg-primary: #0f0f10` → `--background` in shadcn theme
  - `--bg-secondary: #1a1a1c` → `--card` / `--muted`
  - `--accent-primary: #d4a574` → `--primary` (amber accent preserved)
  - `--text-primary: #e8e6e3` → `--foreground`
  - Spacing scale (`--space-xs` through `--space-xl`) → Tailwind spacing utilities
  - Semantic colors (`--success`, `--warning`, `--error`) → custom Tailwind colors
- Keep `xterm.css` (xterm.js's own stylesheet — not ours to replace)
- Font setup: DM Sans + JetBrains Mono configured in Tailwind fontFamily
- Dark mode: shadcn's `dark` class on root (SubFrame is dark-only, so hardcode this)

**Phase 5 — Testing & Cleanup**
- Remove all 20 vanilla renderer `.js` files
- Remove dead HTML markup from index.html
- Remove old CSS files (replaced by Tailwind + shadcn)
- Verify all IPC channels work end-to-end with typed system
- Test xterm.js lifecycle in React (mount, unmount, resize, fit)
- Test TanStack Table sorting/filtering on tasks and sessions
- Test all shadcn components render correctly in Electron (no browser-only APIs)
- Test Framer Motion animations don't cause layout thrashing
- Test all panel toggles, modals, keyboard shortcuts, context menus
- Cross-platform verification (Windows + macOS)
- Update esbuild scripts, dev.js, STRUCTURE.json
- Update CLAUDE.md CSS Design System section to reference Tailwind + shadcn instead of variables.css

**Key technical considerations:**
- xterm.js in React requires careful lifecycle management — Terminal instance must be created in useEffect, disposed on unmount, and fit() called on resize via ResizeObserver
- shadcn/ui in Electron: components are copied into the project (not a node_module), so no CDN or network dependency — works offline
- Tailwind CSS in Electron: PostCSS build step integrates with esbuild via plugin or as a pre-build step
- TanStack Table is headless — shadcn's `<Table>` component provides the styling, TanStack provides the logic (sorting, filtering, column visibility)
- TanStack Query `queryClient.invalidateQueries(['fileTree'])` triggered by fs.watch events from main process = automatic UI refresh
- Framer Motion `AnimatePresence` requires components to have `key` props and `exit` animations — plan component structure accordingly
- node-pty stays server-side (main process) — only the IPC transport changes type signatures
- Electron's contextBridge/preload pattern stays the same — React just calls the exposed API
- The two-phase init (initCritical/initDeferred) maps to React.lazy + Suspense for deferred panels
- D3.js coexists with React via useRef + useEffect (D3 manages its own DOM subtree within a ref'd container)

**Dependency additions:**
- Production: `react`, `react-dom`, `zustand`, `@tanstack/react-query`, `@tanstack/react-table`, `framer-motion`, `class-variance-authority`, `clsx`, `tailwind-merge`, `lucide-react`
- Dev: `typescript`, `@types/react`, `@types/react-dom`, `@types/node`, `tailwindcss`, `postcss`, `autoprefixer`
- shadcn components: installed via CLI into `src/renderer/components/ui/` (not a dependency — source code copied in)

**Files affected:** Every file in src/renderer/ (20 files rewritten to TSX), every file in src/main/ (20 files converted to TS), every file in src/shared/ (6 files converted to TS), all 6 CSS files (deleted/replaced by Tailwind), index.html (simplified), package.json, esbuild config, plus new: tsconfig.json files, tailwind.config.ts, postcss.config.js, components.json (shadcn config), src/renderer/components/ui/ directory. ~52 files total affected.
completed.userRequest: User said: 'I want you to ref our docs/refs etc - Create a proper subtask to implement a full refactor of our UI to use React and TypeScript-etc'. Follow-up: User chose 'Moderate — React + Zustand + TanStack Query' stack, then added: 'plus shadcn and framermotion etc' and 'we should add table so we can add proper sorting/filtering etc cleanly' (TanStack Table).
completed.acceptanceCriteria: 1. TypeScript compiles with strict mode — zero `any` escape hatches in new code. 2. All IPC channels are type-safe (channel name → payload → return type). 3. All 20 renderer modules are React TSX components with proper hooks (no imperative DOM manipulation). 4. xterm.js terminals mount/unmount cleanly in React lifecycle without memory leaks. 5. Zustand stores for UI state — no global mutable state object. 6. TanStack Query wraps all IPC data-fetching calls with proper caching and invalidation. 7. TanStack Table powers tasks list and sessions list with working sort + filter. 8. shadcn/ui components used for all interactive elements (buttons, dialogs, tabs, dropdowns, context menus, toasts, forms). 9. Tailwind CSS is the sole styling system — no hand-written CSS files remain (except xterm.css). 10. SubFrame's warm-neutral + amber accent design preserved in Tailwind theme config. 11. Framer Motion animations on panel transitions, modal open/close, list items. 12. All existing features work identically: multi-terminal (tabs + grid), file tree, editor, all panels (tasks, plugins/sessions, settings, overview, AI files, GitHub), sidebar resize, keyboard shortcuts, workspace switching. 13. Main process fully typed in TypeScript. 14. Shared modules fully typed with exported interfaces. 15. Build still works via esbuild (sub-second builds preserved). 16. Cross-platform: Windows + macOS verified. 17. index.html reduced to minimal shell (React root div + loading screen). 18. No regressions in startup performance (splash screen, deferred panel loading via Suspense).
completed.notes:
This is the largest refactor in SubFrame's history. Full implementation plan written to .subframe/PROJECT_NOTES.md [2026-03-01] section with exact configs, commands, migration order, and risk mitigations.

**Research completed (4-agent team):**
- Renderer: 20 files, 9,100 lines, all DOM patterns + module dependencies mapped
- IPC: 154 channels traced (28 handle, 126 on) — 35 query candidates, 40 mutation candidates, 10 streams
- CSS: 7,158 lines, 41 design tokens, 15 keyframe animations, 913 selectors, full Tailwind mapping
- Stack: shadcn manual setup confirmed (rsc:false), Tailwind v4 via esbuild-plugin-tailwindcss v2.x, TanStack Query wraps ipcRenderer.invoke() directly, React 19 works on Electron 28 (but v28 is EOL — upgrade recommended), Framer Motion hardware-accelerated in Electron, DIY xterm.js hook recommended over wrappers

**Stack decision rationale:**
- **Zustand over Redux/Context**: SubFrame's state is simple UI state + server-derived data. Zustand is ~1KB, zero boilerplate, works outside React (keyboard handlers).
- **TanStack Query for IPC**: Wraps ipcRenderer.invoke() with caching, stale-while-revalidate, loading/error states. queryClient.invalidateQueries() triggered by fs.watch = automatic UI refresh.
- **TanStack Table for lists**: Tasks panel needs sort by priority/status/date + text filter. Sessions panel needs sort by date/state + search. Headless — shadcn provides styled `<Table>` shell.
- **shadcn/ui**: Accessible, themed components copied into project (no runtime dependency). Built on Radix UI primitives. Works offline in Electron.
- **Tailwind v4**: CSS-first config (no tailwind.config.js). @theme directive replaces variables.css. Dark-mode-only simplifies config.
- **Framer Motion**: AnimatePresence enables exit animations (impossible with CSS). Layout animations for sidebar resize.

**Migration order (leaf → root):** state.js → sidebarResize → aiToolSelector → editor → fileTree → projectList → historyPanel → terminal system → all panels → overviewPanel → structureMap → App root.

**Critical finding:** Electron 28 is EOL since Oct 2025 — bundle upgrade to v33+ in Phase 0.

Some existing pending sub-tasks (task-subtask-enhancements, task-subtask-ux, task-terminal-warp) will be significantly easier to implement AFTER this refactor. The SubFrame Server (web app mode) future vision also benefits — React + Tailwind components work identically in Electron and browser.

completed.title: Sub-Task system enhancements (schema + UI)
completed.context: Session 2026-02-28 - Sub-Task system discussion and CLAUDE.md improvements
completed.description:
Multiple improvements to the Sub-Task system. (1) Schema: Add `blockedBy` / `dependsOn` array fields to the task schema, allowing sub-tasks to reference other sub-task IDs they depend on. Update taskSchema in tasks.json, AGENTS.md, CLAUDE.md, and frameTemplates.js. (2) UI - In-Progress visual distinction: The Sub-Tasks panel should visually distinguish `in_progress` sub-tasks from `pending` — e.g., highlighted border, accent color indicator, or a pulsing dot similar to active sessions. (3) UI - Sort by priority: Sub-tasks should sort by priority (high → medium → low) within each status group, so high-priority items surface first. (4) UI - Blocked indicator: If `blockedBy` is populated and those sub-tasks aren't completed, show a blocked badge/icon on the sub-task card. (5) Schema update in templates: Update frameTemplates.js getTasksTemplate() to include the new fields in taskSchema.
(Bonus: Ability to pipe handwritten through AI to format properly per SubFrames task schema cleanly and appropriately without loss)

completed.title: Sub-Task panel & modal UX overhaul
completed.description: Comprehensive UX improvements to the Sub-Task system's UI. (1) Larger modal — increase max-width from 480px to ~640px, give description a proper resizable textarea. (2) Full schema support in modal — add fields for userRequest, acceptanceCriteria, and notes. These are required by the schema but currently only fillable via JSON. Make userRequest and acceptanceCriteria visible in edit mode but read-only for AI-populated content. (3) Detail/expand view — clicking a sub-task card opens a full detail view (inline expansion or larger modal) showing all fields including userRequest, acceptanceCriteria, notes, dates, and context. (4) Description truncation — cap card descriptions at 2-3 lines with expand/collapse toggle. (5) Search/filter by text — add a search input above the filter buttons to filter by title/description text. (6) Keyboard navigation — arrow keys to navigate cards, Enter to expand, 'n' to add new. (7) Send to Claude — button on detail view to copy sub-task context to clipboard or inject into terminal as a prompt. (8) Better validation — replace raw alert() with inline form validation and error styling.

completed.title: System prompt standardization
completed.description: Standardize all system-generated prompts (task delegation, automated commands) to English. Create a centralized prompt template system. frame projesinde oluşturulan içerideki dökümanlarımız, claude md, readme, projectnotes md dosyaları ingilizce olmalı. şu an bu projede bulunan içerik türkçeyse içerik anlamını kaybetmeden ingilizceye çevrilmeli. bundan sonra initialize edilen projelerde bu dosyalarda hazırladığımız direktifler ise ingilizce olarak gelmeli.

completed.title: Tasks UI paneli

completed.title: Warp-style terminal UX improvements
completed.description: Modernize terminal UX inspired by Warp terminal. (1) Smart Ctrl+C — copy to clipboard when text is selected (via terminal.hasSelection()), send SIGINT when no selection. (2) Ctrl+V — intercept before xterm.js and paste from clipboard API (currently requires Ctrl+Shift+V). (3) Right-click context menu — replace current paste-only behavior with a proper context menu: Copy, Paste, Select All, Clear Terminal. (4) Shift+Enter — insert literal newline in multi-line input mode. (5) Scroll-to-bottom overlay — verify existing floating button is polished and visible per-terminal in both tab and grid views. Files: terminalManager.js (key handler rewrite), terminal.css (context menu styles), multiTerminalUI.js (scroll button per-terminal verification).
completed.notes:
[2026-03-03] Reimplemented all terminal UX changes: WebGL/Canvas/Search addons, Framer Motion animated tabs with drag-to-reorder, live output overlay, search bar (Ctrl+F), Shift+Enter newline, animated scroll button, workspace terminal scoping (activeByProject + switchToProject + projectTerminals prop)
[2026-03-07] Implemented: reuse idle terminal (with setting), agent status broadcasting, bot icon in tabs/grid, auto-rename tabs, agent activity full-view popout, multi-session panel/sidebar, session differentiation with timestamps
[2026-03-07] Added session↔terminal correlation via timestamp matching in ptyManager. Jump to terminal button in AgentStateView session cards and full-view detail pane.
